title: Dublin Traceroute (Help Wanted)
author: Pruthviraj1223
body:
Help Wanted.

I'm developing a module that traces the packet from source to destination, especially multiple paths where ECMP, per packet, or per-flow load balancing is applied in the router or hop. I have encountered many commands, but I am stuck on one problem: load balancing happens at the router. I want to know the packet loss and RTT (latency). I have researched a lot about it, but I'm unable to find the packet loss at multiple paths. So can you help me with this? Can Dublin do this or is there any other command/library that does that or any other way to calculate this?  My ultimate goal is to draw a path from source to destination, including a hop with packet loss and RTT and mainly multipath with packet loss and RTT. 

Can anyone help me ?

title: macOS instructions for downloading and installing via homebrew do not work
author: ns-gpilz
body:
Running `brew install https://raw.githubusercontent.com/insomniacslk/dublin-traceroute/master/homebrew/dublin-traceroute.rb` resulted in the following:

`Error: Non-checksummed download of dublin-traceroute formula file from an arbitrary URL is unsupported! `brew extract` or `brew create` and `brew tap-new` to create a formula file in a tap on GitHub instead.`

title: brew install not working for MacOS
author: rahulbahal7
body:
```
$ brew install https://raw.githubusercontent.com/insomniacslk/dublin-traceroute/master/homebrew/dublin-traceroute.rb
Warning: No available formula with the name "ca-certificates".
==> Searching for similarly named formulae...
Error: No similarly named formulae found.
==> Searching for a previously deleted formula (in the last month)...
Warning: homebrew/core is shallow clone. To get its complete history, run:
  git -C "$(brew --repo homebrew/core)" fetch --unshallow

Error: No previously deleted formula found.
==> Searching taps on GitHub...
Warning: Error searching on GitHub: GitHub API Error: Requires authentication
The GitHub credentials in the macOS keychain may be invalid.
Clear them with:
  printf "protocol=https\nhost=github.com\n" | git credential-osxkeychain erase
Create a GitHub personal access token:
    https://github.com/settings/tokens/new?scopes=gist,repo,workflow&description=Homebrew
  echo 'export HOMEBREW_GITHUB_API_TOKEN=your_token_here' >> ~/.zshrc


Error: No formulae found in taps.
Error: 
  homebrew-core is a shallow clone.
  homebrew-cask is a shallow clone.
To `brew update`, first run:
  git -C /usr/local/Homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-core fetch --unshallow
  git -C /usr/local/Homebrew/Library/Taps/homebrew/homebrew-cask fetch --unshallow

These commands may take a few minutes to run due to the large size of the repositories.
This restriction has been made on GitHub's request because updating shallow
clones is an extremely expensive operation due to the tree layout and traffic of
Homebrew/homebrew-core and Homebrew/homebrew-cask. We don't do this for you
automatically to avoid repeatedly performing an expensive unshallow operation in
CI systems (which should instead be fixed to not use shallow clones). Sorry for
the inconvenience!
Error: Non-checksummed download of dublin-traceroute formula file from an arbitrary URL is unsupported! `brew extract` or `brew create` and `brew tap-new` to create a formula file in a tap on GitHub instead.
```

title: Not getting the trace even though "modern traceroute" is getting the trace fine.
author: ghost
body:
So, I have ECMP implemented on my machine is the way that gives different traces for different source ports:

```
traceroute  --version
Modern traceroute for Linux, version 2.1.0
Copyright (c) 2016  Dmitry Butskoy,   License: GPL v2 or any later
sysctl -w net.ipv4.fib_multipath_hash_fields=0x10 # :: use source port
sysctl -w net.ipv4.fib_multipath_hash_policy=0x3  # :: actually use custom multipath fields
sysctl -w net.ipv4.fib_multipath_use_neigh=1 # :: no dead neighbours
ip route add 8.8.8.8 nexthop via 10.0.2.1 nexthop via 192.168.2.1
 
traceroute -M udp -p 80 -U  8.8.8.8 --sport=33074
traceroute -M udp -p 80 -U  8.8.8.8 --sport=33075
```

I am giving `-M udp` in order to match dublin-traceroute's probe algorithm.

I am running dublin-traceroute like this:

```
dublin-traceroute -T --npaths=2 -i --sport=33174 -N --delay=1000 46.23.90.181  > trace.json && ../scripts/to_graphviz.py trace.json &&  feh trace.json.png
```
The above command fails, with both tracer ending in a *.
The aforementioned `modern traceroute` works as expected, with sports 33074 and 33075 giving different traces.

title: does not recognize answers
author: dragonxtek
body:
I'm trying to get the equipment from hop 8 to 10 to identify themselves, but I have 2 problems.
The first is that the same router responds to TTL 8,9 and 10. Does anyone know what kind of configuration is that in the router? or why that happens?
![Selection_117](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10552658/137988276-57c499c9-5932-4b21-9ded-df84b183b5aa.jpg)

The second problem is that, although I receive a response, dublin-traceroute does not show anything. Why is that?
![Selection_116](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10552658/137988313-86290498-36a9-4068-9279-e515a71cbbda.jpg)

title: Go Version Build Instructions?
author: haloss1
body:
Hi, first of all, wonderful tool!, i tried installing/building the Go version for IPV6 support from these instructions, yet i'm getting the following errors:
```
dublin-traceroute/go/dublintraceroute/cmd/dublin-traceroute > go get github.com/insomniacslk/dublin-traceroute/go/dublintraceroute/cmd/dublin-traceroute
go get: module github.com/insomniacslk/dublin-traceroute@upgrade found (v0.4.2), but does not contain package github.com/insomniacslk/dublin-traceroute/go/dublintraceroute/cmd/dublin-traceroute
```

```
dublin-traceroute/go/dublintraceroute/cmd/dublin-traceroute > go get -u ./... && go build
go: cannot find main module, but found .git/config in /home/haloss1/Dev/dublin-traceroute
        to create a module there, run:
        cd ../../../.. && go mod init
```

I'm not very familiar with Go, any help is greatly appreciated, i'm doing a comparison of the routes taken from various ISPs here where i live, your tool has helped me a lot visualizing my ISPs infrastructure in IPV4, beign able to see the route in IPV6 would help me diagnose an issue i have regarding their prefix delegation method.
Thank you!

title: Using wrong interface
author: eddie4
body:
Hello let me start to thank you for this tool. 

However i seem to run into an issue where it chooses the wrong source IP. When multiple are assigned to the networkcard. 


Routing table:
```
root@web05:~# route -n
Kernel IP routing table
Destination     Gateway         Genmask         Flags Metric Ref    Use Iface
0.0.0.0         87.233.x.x   0.0.0.0         UG    0      0        0 ens3
10.233.x.0    0.0.0.0         255.255.255.0   U     0      0        0 ens3
10.233.x.0    0.0.0.0         255.255.255.0   U     0      0        0 ens3
87.233.x.x   0.0.0.0         255.255.255.240 U     0      0        0 ens3
```

root@web05:~# ip a
```
2: ens3: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 56:16:d1:5b:58:fd brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 87.233.x.x/28 brd 87.233.x.x scope global ens3
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet 10.233.x.x/24 brd 10.233.x.x scope global ens3
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet 10.233.x.x/24 brd 10.233.x.x scope global ens3
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::5416:d1ff:fe5b:58fd/64 scope link
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```


dublin-traceroute 87.233.y.y  Trace.json
```
                "33453" :
                [
                        {
                                "flowhash" : 34136,
                                "is_last" : true,
                                "name" : "",
                                "received" : null,
                                "rtt_usec" : null,
                                "sent" :
                                {
                                        "ip" :
                                        {
                                                "dst" : "87.233.y.y",
                                                "src" : "10.233.x.x",
                                                "ttl" : 1
                                        },
                                        "timestamp" : "1597074875.919802",
                                        "udp" :
                                        {
                                                "dport" : 33453,
                                                "sport" : 12345
                                        }
                                }
                        }
                ]
        }
```

This does not seem to happen when using two network cards. Just when one networkcard has multiple IP's.

title: Assess usability of go.pkt for the Go implementation
author: insomniacslk
body: Currently I use a custom, partial implementation of IPv4/IPv6/UDP headers. Check `go.pkt` out, https://github.com/ghedo/go.pkt

title: Run dublin-traceroute in container hang
author: shufanhao
body:
Start container command: 
```
docker run -itd --name test --net=host -v $PWD/output:/output insomniacslk/dublin-traceroute /bin/sh
```
Hang here, wait about 1 hours, there is no updated. 
```
dublin-traceroute 220.181.38.148 -d 443
WARNING: you are running this program as root. Consider setting the CAP_NET_RAW
         capability and running as non-root user as a more secure alternative.
Starting dublin-traceroute
Traceroute from 0.0.0.0:12345 to 220.181.38.148:443~462 (probing 20 paths, min TTL is 1, max TTL is 30, delay is 10 ms)
```

Is there anyone who know what's wrong about it ?
